---
title: "BIOL 275 Lab 2 Workflow and Data Types"
format: typst
output-file: lab-02-worksheet.pdf
---

## Setup and workflow

1\. Write down the name of the workspace you joined and confirm you can see the course projects/templates.

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 0.55in)
```
2\. What is the name of your Lab 2 project?

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 0.55in)
```
## Packages and the penguins dataset

3\. What is the difference between installing a package and loading a package?

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 1in)
```
## Data frames, tibbles, and penguins

4\. What does a row represent in the penguins dataset? What does a column represent?

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 1in)
```
## Inspecting a dataset and identifying variable types

5\. How many rows and columns are in penguins?

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 0.55in)
```
6\. From glimpse(penguins), list three variables in the penguins dataset.

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 0.55in)
#pagebreak()
```
7\. Which species is most common in the dataset? How many rows are there for that species?

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 0.55in)
```
8\. Choose two variables in penguins and write their type using (a) lecture terminology and (b) R type from `glimpse()`.

## Summarizing variables

9\. Use `summary(penguins)` to answer: Which variable(s) show missing values?

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 0.55in)
```
## Thinking about relationships between variables

10\. Sketch your prediction by: Drawing x- and y-axes; Labeling the axes bill length and bill depth; Drawing a line or pattern that shows how you think the two variables are related

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 3in)
```
11\. Briefly interpret the plot: What is the relationship between bill length and bill depth? Is that relationship the same across species?

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 1.5in)
```
